name: expiringdict

metadata.name: expiringdict
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: Apache Software License
metadata.summary: Dictionary with auto-expiring values for caching purposes

documentation:
# Expiring Dict

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/mailgun/expiringdict.svg?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/mailgun/expiringdict
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/mailgun/expiringdict/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/mailgun/expiringdict?branch=master
```

[ChangeLog]

expiringdict is a Python caching library. The core of the library is ExpiringDict class which
is an ordered dictionary with auto-expiring values for caching purposes. Expiration happens on
any access, object is locked during cleanup from expired values. ExpiringDict can not store
more than `max_len` elements - the oldest will be deleted.

**Note:** Iteration over dict and also keys() do not remove expired values!

# Installation

If you wish to install from PyPi:

```bash
pip install expiringdict
```

If you wish to download the source and install from GitHub:

```bash
git clone git@github.com:mailgun/expiringdict.git
python setup.py install
```

or to install with test dependencies ([Nose](http://readthedocs.org/docs/nose/en/latest/), [Mock](http://www.voidspace.org.uk/python/mock/), [coverage](http://nedbatchelder.com/code/coverage/)) run from the directory above:

```bash
pip install -e expiringdict[test]
```

To run tests with coverage:

```bash
nosetests --with-coverage --cover-package=expiringdict
```

# Usage

Create a dictionary with capacity for 100 elements and elements expiring in 10 seconds:

```py
from expiringdict import ExpiringDict
cache = ExpiringDict(max_len=100, max_age_seconds=10)
```

put and get a value there:

```py
cache["key"] = "value"
cache.get("key")
```

copy from dict or OrderedDict:

```py
from expiringdict import ExpiringDict
my_dict=dict()
my_dict['test'] = 1
cache = ExpiringDict(max_len=100, max_age_seconds=10, items=my_dict)
assert cache['test'] == 1
```

copy from another ExpiringDict, with or without new length and timeout:

```py
from expiringdict import ExpiringDict
cache_hour = ExpiringDict(max_len=100, max_age_seconds=3600)
cache_hour['test'] = 1
cache_hour_copy = ExpiringDict(max_len=None, max_age_seconds=None, items=cache_hour)
cache_minute_copy = ExpiringDict(max_len=None, max_age_seconds=60, items=cache_hour)
assert cache_minute_copy['test'] == 1
```

pickle :

```py
import dill
from expiringdict import ExpiringDict
cache = ExpiringDict(max_len=100, max_age_seconds=10)
cache['test'] = 1
pickled_cache = dill.dumps(cache)
unpickled_cache = dill.loads(cache)
assert unpickled_cache['test'] == 1
```

[changelog]: ./CHANGELOG.rst

modules.name: expiringdict
documentation:
Dictionary with auto-expiring values for caching purposes.

Expiration happens on any access, object is locked during cleanup from expired
values. Can not store more than max_len elements - the oldest will be deleted.

```pycon
>>> ExpiringDict(max_len=100, max_age_seconds=10)
```

The values stored in the following way:
{

> key1: (value1, created_time1),
> key2: (value2, created_time2)

}

NOTE: iteration over dict and also keys() do not remove expired values!

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.__contains__
documentation: Return True if the dict has a key, else return False.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.__getitem__
documentation:
Return the item of the dict.

Raises a KeyError if key is not in the map.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.pop
documentation:
Get item from the dict and remove it.

Return default if expired or does not exist. Never raise KeyError.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.ttl
documentation:
Return TTL of the `key` (in seconds).

Returns None for non-existent or expired keys.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.get
documentation: Return the value for key if key is in the dictionary, else default.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.items
documentation: Return a copy of the dictionary's list of (key, value) pairs.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.items_with_timestamp
documentation: Return a copy of the dictionary's list of (key, value, timestamp) triples.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.values
documentation:
Return a copy of the dictionary's list of values.
See the note for dict.items().

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.fromkeys
documentation: Create a new dictionary with keys from seq and values set to value.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.iteritems
documentation: Return an iterator over the dictionary's (key, value) pairs.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.itervalues
documentation: Return an iterator over the dictionary's values.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.viewitems
documentation: Return a new view of the dictionary's items ((key, value) pairs).

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.viewkeys
documentation: Return a new view of the dictionary's keys.

methods.name: expiringdict.ExpiringDict.viewvalues
documentation: Return a new view of the dictionary's values.
